spec_match.rationale: Multiple field configurations, an explicit Arabic `dir=rtl` input, and string/two-value `text-overflow` examples with `CSS.supports()` readouts expose the clip-on-focus behaviour and fallback path.

interactive.rationale: Six real focusable inputs let visitors click, type, and navigate through normal, password, readonly, RTL, custom-string, and two-value overflow cases.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Blurb promises diverse field types and configurations, and the page now renders real RTL plus detected custom marker configurations.

edge_cases.rationale: Readonly, password, true RTL, string marker, and two-value marker cases are covered, with support readouts when marker syntax falls back; explicit keyboard-caret instrumentation and a clip baseline remain minor follow-ups.

design_system.rationale: Variables-only colours, no glyph icons, no raw hex.

openQuestions.title: Show keyboard caret navigation explicitly
openQuestions.detail: Pure click-focus triggers the clip. The spec also triggers on arrow-key caret nav. Add a label saying `press End` and a guide bubble that highlights when the caret moves into the clipped region.

openQuestions.title: Compare with text-overflow: clip baseline
openQuestions.detail: Add a fifth field that uses `text-overflow: clip` (no ellipsis) so visitors see what the new on-focus state collapses to — that's the static baseline against which the change is described.

summary: Useful breadth across field types with a good ChromeStatus reference. The moderate RTL and two-value syntax gaps are addressed; remaining work is minor caret-navigation guidance and a static clip baseline.
